Composition.Programmer 1:
Implement backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all ten pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend templates.
- Handle reading and writing of all data files in the 'data/' directory using plain text, starting from the first line, without encryption.
- Process user inputs and interactions for all functionalities  browsing destinations, planning itineraries, searching accommodations, booking flights, viewing travel packages, managing trips, booking confirmations, and travel recommendations.
- Frontend templates to be supported  dashboard.html, destinations.html, destination_details.html, itinerary_planning.html, accommodations.html, transportation.html, travel_packages.html, trip_management.html, booking_confirmation.html, travel_recommendations.html.
- Ensure all routes and backend logic align with the original task requirements and data formats.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Create all HTML templates corresponding to the ten pages specified.
- Each template must include the defined page elements with their respective IDs and structure as per the requirements document.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be implemented as Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Templates to develop  dashboard.html, destinations.html, destination_details.html, itinerary_planning.html, accommodations.html, transportation.html, travel_packages.html, trip_management.html, booking_confirmation.html, travel_recommendations.html.
Composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Implement CSS styles in static/css/styles.css to define the look and feel of the entire web application.
- Implement JavaScript in static/js/scripts.js to handle client-side interactivity such as button clicks, filtering, and dynamic content updates.
- Organize and include images in static/images/ for destinations and travel packages as needed.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
